title: EGA Cohort metadata schema
description: A group of Biomaterials (e.g., individuals, biopsies) sharing specific characteristics, facilitating comparative studies and discovery.
$comment: We need to review that Cohorts can contain high-level (non granular) coverage of the biomaterials: populationCoverage, spatialCoverage and temporalCoverage. @Context for Beacon's entities is unfinished. Just a draft of the root properties for now. Ideally they'll have the context of their schemas embedded in them, so we can reference. Not quite there yet though.

allOf.title: Compatibility with Beacon v2 JSON Schema
allOf.$comment: #! In the future we should remove the requirement of the root 'id', as we are using the object identifier naming of '@id', more RDF-native. (1) We can remove this 'cohort as a whole' reference to Beacon's model, and add individual property references -- Tried it, but Biovalidator would not be able to insert 'async' true to all nested refs, which would require a code change; (2) leave both 'id' and '@id' at root; (3) replace the EGA's '@id' naming with 'id', and instead just depend on the existing 'id' -> '@id' alias in the context.json file for RDF expansion.

@type.title: Cohort resource type
@type.description: The JSON-LD type for an EGA cohort.
